briefdescription.para: This function handles TX buffer interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: It is called by the interrupt when a transmit complete interrupt occurs. It returns buffers of completed descriptors to the caller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the Bit mask of endpoints that caused a transmit complete interrupt.

briefdescription.para: This function handles RX buffer interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: It is called by the interrupt when a receive complete interrupt occurs. It notifies the callback functions that have been registered with the individual endpoints that data has been received.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
Bit mask of endpoints that caused a receive complete interrupt.

briefdescription.para: This function handles a RESET interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: It will notify the interrupt handler callback of the RESET condition.

InstancePtr
is pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller
IrqSts
is the Interrupt status register content. To be passed on to the user.
None None.

briefdescription.para: This function handles a Setup Packet interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: It will notify the interrupt handler callback of the RESET condition.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
None None

briefdescription.para: This function is the first-level interrupt handler for the USB core.

detaileddescription.para: All USB interrupts will be handled here. Depending on the type of the interrupt, second level interrupt handler may be called. Second level interrupt handlers will be registered by the user using the: XUsbPs_IntrSetHandler() and/or XUsbPs_EpSetHandler() functions.

parameterdescription.para: is a Reference passed to the interrupt register function. In our case this will be a pointer to the XUsbPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function registers the user callback handler for controller (non-endpoint) interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsbPs instance of the controller.
is the Callback function to register. CallBackFunc may be NULL to clear the entry.
is the user data reference passed to the callback function. CallBackRef may be NULL.
is the User interrupt mask. Defines which interrupts will cause the callback to be called.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE: Callback could not be registered.
